summary: The arbitrary file read vulnerability was successfully confirmed. The invoice import preview functionality (plugins/importFE_ZIP/view.php and plugins/importFE/view.php) uses user-controlled filename parameter in file_get_contents() without path validation, allowing path traversal attacks.
expected_security_impact: Authenticated users can read arbitrary files on the server, including configuration files with database credentials, source code, and system files.
actual_observed_result: The test script analyzed both vulnerable files and confirmed the presence of file_get_contents() calls with user-controlled filename parameter without sanitization. The pattern file_get_contents($directory.'/'.get('filename')) allows path traversal via '../../../' sequences.
verification_method: Automated code analysis verifies the presence of unsafe file_get_contents() pattern with user-controlled filename parameter. Exit code 1 indicates vulnerability confirmation.
evidence_produced_by_run: file_get_contents() with unsanitized filename parameter found in plugins/importFE_ZIP/view.php
Same vulnerable pattern found in plugins/importFE/view.php
No path validation or sanitization before file access
User-controlled GET parameter 'filename' directly concatenated with directory path
Exit code 1 returned indicating vulnerability confirmed

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
